data.epi_content:
When I opened the door, Evan looked at me with a face that seemed ready to cry.

I was bewildered.

I hadn't done anything yet, but he was showing me a different attitude.

Looking at me with eyes full of sadness or love-hate rather than anger.

Whenever that happens, I just feel confused and afraid.

I had assumed the world would always flow the same way, so when it changes, it's naturally confusing.

What's different and unfamiliar usually comes across as repulsive.

People tend to enjoy pushing away and rejecting what they don't know rather than trying to understand it.

But Evan wasn't someone I found repulsive.

Even though he had changed into something different and unfamiliar, Evan was still Evan.

Sometimes I wonder if this friend, like me, is living a life struggling in pain.

Yes. Only Evan changes.

As if he alone is special among the countless people moving identically in this repeating time.

"Right, we haven't faced each other for a while, so I wonder why you came."

"Should we go inside and talk?"

At those words, I thought for a moment and looked around my room.

It wasn't messy, but it wasn't clean enough to welcome a guest either.

"Wait a moment.

Since you came suddenly, I can at least do that much."

"...Alright."

After entering the room, I moved the table to the center and arranged the chairs to face each other.

I put some tea leaves in the teapot, poured hot water, gathered the various papers on the desk into one place, and then walked back to the entrance to open the door.

"It's ready. You can come in now."

After entering my room, Evan looked around with a face full of nostalgia.

Especially, his gaze lingered for quite a while on the short dagger placed as decoration.

"What's wrong?"

"Do you know why I came here?"

"Yes, you probably heard about me slapping Vivian, thought about it for a while, and then came looking for me in anger."

"Yes, until just now I was extremely angry.

Sad emotions, indifferent memories, and disappointment in Vivian, you could say.

Suddenly those feelings welled up, and I just found myself walking here.

I felt like if I came to you, I could find the answer."

Hearing those words, rather than answering, I took a sip of tea first.

Evan's eyes had a slight taste to them.

Eyes that have become numb after living too long in a place where bullets rain down, taking too many lives—sunken, haggard eyes with a glimmer of light.

Had he killed many people in between?

It hadn't even been a few hours since I slapped Vivian.

"Hey Evan, do you remember?

When we grabbed each other's collars here, cursed each other's families, slapped faces, pointed fingers, pushed, and spat out words too difficult to even speak, words we didn't even want to say to each other."

At those words, Evan slightly frowned and then closed his mouth.

As if there were scenes coming to mind, but he wasn't sure if his memories were accurate.

"I, I..."

"You look thirsty, have some tea first."

I pour tea into Evan's empty cup.

Then I took out some chocolate from the drawer and placed it on a plate.
data.epi_content2:
"Why are you the only special one?

Why are you the only one special to me?"

"What are you saying..."

"Just nonsense, meaningless nonsense.

Hey Evan. Why are you the only special one?"

He just looks at me with confused eyes.

I sigh once and then brush it off as if it's nothing.

"It's nothing."

Then Evan awkwardly reaches out with trembling hands, trying to caress my cheek.

I push his hand away and shove him back.

He looks at me with a slightly dumbfounded face.

Seeing that, I become slightly amused and burst into laughter.

"Hey Evan, shall we go on a date after so long?"

Evan didn't answer.

Maybe, just maybe. He might have received some hint from the future.

Or perhaps the emotions he felt seeing me dead remain in this vicinity.

I'd like that.

I thought there wasn't a single person who thought of me as special or precious, but at least there's one person left.

But his pupils are shaking.

As if, like when I looked at Evan before, thinking he wouldn't act like this.

He wouldn't behave this way.

"I'm joking."

To brush it off, I gulp down the full cup of tea and pour hot water again to brew more.

My palate was completely scalded, making the outer skin feel slightly mushy.

As if my sense of pain had returned, there was also a slight stinging sensation.

I quickly cover my mouth.

The thought that Evan might be coming back like me, or following me back, made me start to feel nauseous.

Whether it's because of complicated feelings, or because I find Evan who now thinks of me, or thinks of me as precious, disgusting, or because of this world that seems to hate me, or perhaps because I hate Vivian who can snatch this person in front of me and then discard him as useless, or because of this lady's emotions in my body.

I don't know.

"Evan, I think it would be good if you left my room for now.

After fighting with Vivian today, I'm feeling quite depressed."

"But..."

"I said leave."

Even with the order to leave, Evan just looks bewildered and doesn't go.

And I realized that not much has changed in the world.

Evan still isn't looking at me.

Perhaps, it's not "still" but "not yet."

"Evan. I'm not someone you need to protect, I'm just a fool who slapped Vivian's cheek and is now feeling sorry for myself.

If you're not going to jump up from your seat, point fingers at me, and criticize how inadequate and miserable a person I am, then please leave right now."

Every time I died, Evan gradually changed.

For some reason, he would look at me affectionately, and at the moment of my death, he would show regret for his mistakes and weep bitterly.

In the first place, if he was going to be that sad, he wouldn't have treated me that way.

If I had taken up such a large place in his heart, he wouldn't have treated me like that.

He wouldn't have pushed me away when I approached him.

Who knows.
data.epi_content3:
Anyway, isn't it enough to imprint it on him?

Even if time goes back after I die, I'll imprint something memorable on him.

Something even that stupid, bookish gentleman can easily remember.

Death itself is a simple yet intense event.

But this lady has asked me not to commit suicide.

"Evan, please leave for today.

We'll continue attending the Academy, so we'll have plenty of chances to meet."

I pour green tea into Evan's empty cup.

As the tea fills the cup, it begins to reflect light and emit a green glow.

Even though the cup is full, I continue pouring.

Soon the tea overflows onto the table, dripping drop by drop, and now it starts to pour onto Evan's pants.

"Look at this cup."

If he doesn't listen to what I say, it seems sufficient to show it through action.

See, even a metaphor becomes so focused when shown through action.

It's just like that.

I haven't even heard about my family's downfall yet, and he treats me so easily.

Then how will I appear after my family falls?

Just someone who can't do anything, who can't say a word of complaint no matter what is done to them, who can be easily subdued despite struggling...

Just a pretty and cute doll with the premium mark of once being a noble.

"It's overflowing.

When attention overflows too, it becomes burdensome and makes you want to spit it all out.

It's getting dark, go home, Evan."

Only then does Evan look at me with a slightly dazed face before getting up and leaving the room.

I also stood up to see him off.

After watching Evan leave the dormitory room for quite a while, I slightly squeezed my eyes shut, grabbed the gold coins in my room, and went out for a walk.

Probably by now, there would be no chance of running into the Crown Prince, so it wouldn't matter if I went to a suitable store to buy tea or tobacco.

Come to think of it, my waist feels empty.

I rummaged through the drawer to take out the pistol, and with familiar hand movements, I inserted the bullet.

"Heheh."

At first, I couldn't even properly insert bullets and always dropped them.

The fact that I've become accustomed to it now makes me laugh.

Since I didn't want to accidentally create a hole in my thigh, I didn't cock the hammer but tucked the pistol into my waistband before leaving the room.

A cold and refreshing, yet slightly stinging wind brushes past my cheeks.

The kind of wind that might make the tip of my nose slightly red.

It would be nice if it were soon buried in snow, but in the time I'm trapped in, it never snows at all.

Occasionally, frozen ice might fall, but that's it.

Magic is such an inhuman thing, terribly manipulating nature at will.

With even the twilight having fled, outside is just dark.

I enter a store, buy some tobacco leaves and a pipe, and then buy lots of food before returning to my room.

How many days has it been?

It was probably the time when all the chocolate was eaten up.

If an insignificant, worthless person were to harm a brilliant magician, a girl loved by the world.

No, since I couldn't possibly do anything against her, if I were to try to harm her.

Perhaps, that itself would be quite an impressive act.

That's what I thought.

How disgusting. Haha!
